macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: No Major Macroeconomic or Stock Market News Found for Past 24 Hours
items.summary: Search results contain no relevant macroeconomic, Fed, central bank, earnings, or index movement news from the past 24 hours; the only result is an unrelated TikTok video about last-minute gift shopping.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $62K as HYPE and ADA Lead Weekend Altcoin Recovery
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $62,500 (+1.3% daily, +3.6% weekly) while Ethereum surged over 11% weekly to $1,754; Hyperliquid's HYPE and Cardano's ADA posted the strongest daily gains, exceeding 6%.

items.title: US Treasury Sanctions 100+ ISIS-K Crypto Addresses Linked to $1.4M in Funds
items.summary: The U.S. Treasury sanctioned over 100 ISIS-K crypto addresses tied to $1.4 million in funds moved via TRX, XMR, and BTC, marking a significant regulatory action against illicit crypto activity.

items.title: Senator Gillibrand Proposes Ban on Officials Issuing Personal Crypto Tokens
items.summary: Senator Gillibrand introduced a proposal to ban U.S. officials from issuing or sponsoring personal crypto tokens, raising regulatory risks for token launches, fundraising, and CEX/DeFi adoption.

items.title: Global Insurance Broker Aon Tests Stablecoin Payments for Insurance Premiums
items.summary: Aon, a global insurance broker, is piloting stablecoin payments for insurance premiums in collaboration with Coinbase and Paxos, signaling growing institutional adoption of stablecoins.

items.title: Nasdaq and Kraken Partner to Launch Tokenized Stock Trading
items.summary: Nasdaq and Kraken announced a partnership to launch trading for tokenized stocks, expanding crypto infrastructure for traditional asset tokenization.

items.title: Total Crypto Market Cap Rebounds to $2.2T After Early-Month Volatility
items.summary: The total cryptocurrency market capitalization surpassed $2.2 trillion, recovering from early-month volatility, with Bitcoin dominance remaining steady at 57.8% despite broad altcoin gains.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Returns with Groundbreaking Autonomous Coding and Reasoning Capabilities
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 has been restored to public access, delivering frontier AI performance in autonomous coding, long-horizon reasoning, and vision while supporting million-token contexts and multi-day projects with minimal supervision.

items.title: Google NotebookLM Becomes Agentic Free AI Tool for Automated Work
items.summary: Google quietly updated NotebookLM to become an agentic AI tool that now performs actual work for users, making it one of the most powerful free AI tools available.

items.title: AI Regulatory History Ends with New Government-Integrated Pre-Release Model for Frontier AI
items.summary: Following Claude Fable 5's restoration, a new regulatory framework has emerged where government is now inside the pre-release process for frontier models, with faster jailbreak information sharing and expanded independent testing access.

items.title: Massive $700B Investment in AI Infrastructure by Amazon, Google, and Berkshire
items.summary: Amazon, Google, and Berkshire are pouring $700 billion into AI infrastructure, with major investments in training data centers, LLM development, and companies like Nokia and Nvidia.

items.title: Escalating Tensions Over AI Data Centers Lead to First Data Center Moratorium in Massachusetts
items.summary: Lowell, Massachusetts enacted the state's first data center moratorium in spring 2026 due to energy consumption concerns, with tensions escalating after a 14-year-old girl was detained for speaking out at a zoning forum.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Announces Mass Layoffs Amid Sony/PlayStation Industry Shift
items.summary: Bungie has officially announced mass layoffs following reports of financial mismanagement, with Sony and PlayStation commenting on the situation ahead of the Destiny 2: Renegades expansion launch.

items.title: Xbox Expected to Announce Cataclysmic Layoffs in Gaming Sector
items.summary: Industry rumors suggest Xbox is about to announce massive layoffs, potentially signaling a broader collapse in the gaming space following Bungie's recent layoffs.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals New Force of Law Warbond for Cooperative Shooter
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprised fans by announcing the upcoming Force of Law Warbond, adding new content to the popular cooperative shooter experience.

items.title: Sony Announces New PlayStation Controller with Unexpected Design
items.summary: During the PlayStation State of Play, Sony revealed a new controller featuring an unexpected design, marking a notable hardware announcement for PS5 users.

items.title: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo Announce Nioh 3 at Sony State of Play
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo officially announced Nioh 3 during Sony's June State of Play showcase, offering immediate entry for soulslike fans.

items.title: Pokemon GO Brings Back Community Day Voting for August 2025
items.summary: Pokemon GO has reintroduced Community Day voting for its upcoming August 2025 edition, with four Pokemon candidates for the spotlight event.

items.title: FURIA vs LYON G2 Highlights Round 1 LoL MSI 2026 Main Stage
items.summary: The second game of FURIA vs LYON at Round 1 LoL MSI 2026 Main Stage in Korea showcased competitive League of Legends gameplay under Patch 26.13.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Debuts as Most Advanced Foldable Yet
items.summary: Samsung launched its Galaxy Z TriFold, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M for 100-Hour Battery Development
items.summary: Battery maker Form Energy announced $405 million in funding to accelerate the development of its long-awaited 100-hour battery, a longer-lasting alternative to lithium batteries.

items.title: X Returns to Brazil After Month-Long Suspension
items.summary: Elon Musk's social media platform X is back in Brazil after complying with all judicial demands following a suspension that lasted over a month due to clashes with the country's highest court.

items.title: Northvolt Head of Main Plant to Step Down
items.summary: Battery maker Northvolt announced that the head of its main plant will step down, marking a leadership change amid the company's ongoing operations.

items.title: American Water Cyberattack Highlights Critical Infrastructure Risks
items.summary: A continuing cyberattack on American Water, the nation's largest regulated water utility, has renewed focus on the urgent need to protect critical infrastructure from digital threats.

items.title: US Considers Breaking Up Google in Antitrust Case
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to force Google to sell parts of its business to eliminate its online search monopoly.

items.title: Space Startups See Funding Growth as Investors Bet on Capex Ventures
items.summary: A new report indicates that space startups are experiencing increased funding as investors increasingly bet on capex-intensive ventures in the sector.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Quantum Computer Using Standard Silicon
items.summary: Quantum Motion has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer built using standard silicon chip fabrication, a milestone that could accelerate the development of scalable quantum systems.
